_source: Household systems & appliances: manufacturer owner's-manual guidance aggregated by the U.S. Department of Energy 'Energy Saver' maintenance checklists (energy.gov) and ENERGY STAR HVAC maintenance guidance (filter changes, annual tune-ups); NFPA 211 / U.S. Fire Administration guidance on annual dryer-vent cleaning (fire-prevention basis); National Roofing Contractors Association (NRCA) and This Old House guidance on annual roof inspection and twice-yearly (spring/fall) gutter cleaning; typical manufacturer care-guide intervals (GE, Whirlpool, Samsung, LG) for refrigerator coil cleaning and water-filter replacement, dishwasher filter/spray-arm cleaning, and washer hose/gasket checks. Cost ranges are 2024-2025 U.S. national-average service-call ranges per Angi and HomeAdvisor cost guides — treat as ballpark, not a quote. Vehicle tasks: typical manufacturer maintenance-schedule intervals (commonly 5,000-7,500 mi for conventional oil, ~annual for synthetic; 6,000 mi tire rotation; annual brake/battery/cabin-filter check), converted to calendar cadence using the FHWA/Federal Highway Administration average of ~13,500 miles driven per year in the U.S. — Phase 1 schedules are calendar-based only (see docs/ontology.md maintenance_schedules), so mileage intervals are approximated as time. Vehicle service cost ranges per RepairPal and Angi national averages. These are defaults meant to be edited per household, not authoritative recommendations for a specific make/model.

water_heater.description: DIY drain is free; a plumber service call typically runs $75-$150

refrigerator.description: DIY vacuum is free; a service call runs roughly $75-$150

washer.description: Preventive replacement reduces flood risk even if hoses look fine

dryer.description: Lint-fire prevention (NFPA 211 / U.S. Fire Administration guidance)

vehicle.description: Only applies where your state/locality requires it
